calculated_metrics.confidence_note: This unit cost is estimated by PRISM by dividing total programme expenditure by the total quantified animal beneficiaries.

details.calculation: ($2,512,356 USD / 13,476 total beneficiaries) = $186.43 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 5 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

calculated_metrics.confidence_note: IES is a Back-of-the-Envelope Calculation (BOTEC) to standardize impact across interventions and species. It relies on philosophical and empirical assumptions.

details.criteria: Pass: <= 2 years of expenditure. | Warn: 2-5 years. | Fail: > 5 years.
Pass: >= 6 months of operating expenses. | Warn: 3-6 months. | Fail: < 3 months.
Pass: At least one intervention is classified as Tier 1 (Systemic Change) or Tier 2 (Prevention & Scalable Care). | Warn: All verifiable interventions are Tier 3 (Direct Care & Indirect Action).

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.calculation: No valid counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.criteria: Pass: At least one metric has a quantified counterfactual baseline. | Warn: A qualitative baseline is provided, but lacks quantified data. | Fail: No valid counterfactual baseline is provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline with a source quote.

details.criteria: Pass: Government grants are < 80% of total income. | Fail: Government grants are >= 80% of total income.

details.calculation: Mixed portfolio with minority focus on high-neglectedness areas (Farmed Animals: 0%, Wild Animals: 0%, Companion Animals: 0%, Unspecified: 100%).
details.criteria: Pass: >= 50% of beneficiaries are high-neglectedness (farmed/wild). | Warn: 1-49% are high-neglectedness. | Fail: 0% are high-neglectedness.
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: No disclosure of unintended negative impacts found. This is the industry norm.
details.criteria: Bonus: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found.
details.elaboration: This check rewards epistemic humility. Non-disclosure is standard and not a failure.
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.
details.criteria: Bonus: Euthanasia/live-release data is disclosed. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found. | N/A: Not applicable for non-sheltering organisations.
details.elaboration: This check rewards transparency for a sensitive operational metric.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

source.quote:
Management and General Support 1.87%
Total Operating Expenses 23,04,81,380

Membership Development 4.87%

Direct Programme Support 93.26%

Contributions ₹ 23,86,66,200
Total Revenue *23,94,05,260
Total Operating Expenses 23,04,81,380

Net Assets End of Year Unrestricted ₹2,98,65,610
*Net assets include fixed assets of ₹1,24,08,360.
Net Assets End of Year Unrestricted: ₹2,98,65,610

Our emergency helpline, which receives more than 1,400 calls every day about dogs who have been hit by cars, cats who have fallen into drains, and other harmful incidents, handled nearly 3,000 remote rescue cases
this year alone our Rapid Rescue Team tended to an astonishing 10,000 on-the-ground emergency cases in Mumbai and Delhi
save more than 30 stranded dogs and buffaloes, along with a cow, in Delhi NCR.
rescuing over 300 dogs, cats, sugar gliders, hedgehogs, and other animals and relocating them to safety.
We helped save seven camels from sacrifice ahead of Eid
secured the rescue of a 36-year-old elephant named Madhuri, also known as Mahadevi, from decades of being in chains
successfully encouraged ISKCON Mayapur in West Bengal to release two other elephants, Bishnupriya and Lakshmipriya
PETA India raided North East Sun Circus and seized three doves and a goat who were being illegally forced to perform.
PETA India raided North East Sun Circus and seized three doves and a goat who were being illegally forced to perform.
We aided officials in halting more than 100 unlawful animal fights - saving bulls, rams, dogs, and roosters from these bloody spectacles

Working with the guardians of two dogs who endured severe beatings at a "pet" boarding and training business that left one of the dogs partially blind, we helped get the place shut down. All the dogs were returned to their homes.
confiscate gravely ill working animals and had 30 injured, emaciated horses reportedly used to pull carriages confiscated.
PETA India to the Rescue!Working to Protect Animalkind
PETA India's Cruelty Response Team handled more than 6,000 crime cases nationwide

metrics.metric_name: Students reached through humane education workshops

source.quote: PETA India and partner organisations presented our Compassionate Citizen programme in 4,591 humane education workshops to 861,640 students.
animatronic elephant Ellie, voiced by actor Dia Mirza, taught more than 50,000 children in schools across India that elephants suffer when forced to give rides.
this year alone our Rapid Rescue Team tended to an astonishing 10,000 on-the-ground emergency cases in Mumbai and Delhi
PETA India's swift intervention kept thousands of chicks from being hurled onto rooftops in an abusive ritual at a temple in Belagavi.
donated vegan biryani to 1,000 people in need for Eid in Hyderabad

metrics.metric_name: Students reached through PETA Youth events

source.quote: PETA Youth reached thousands more students with virtual reality experiences, interactive lectures, and other outreach at 32 events.

metrics.metric_name: Personnel trained in animal protection laws

source.quote: over 2,000 police and animal husbandry personnel attended our 25 workshops on animal protection laws
handled nearly 3,000 remote rescue cases
rescuing over 300 dogs, cats, sugar gliders, hedgehogs, and other animals and relocating them to safety.
Our work led authorities to bust and cancel more than 200 illegal races, preventing horses, bullocks, rams, greyhounds, donkeys, and pigeons from being injured or killed.
over 200 complaints were filed against animal abusers.
inspectors recommended the rescue of 1,200+ animals from inside this hellhole.
We aided officials in halting more than 100 unlawful animal fights - saving bulls, rams, dogs, and roosters from these bloody spectacles
seized hundreds of prohibited spiked bits designed to control horses through pain
rescued hundreds of birds from lives of imprisonment from various markets - leading to arrests.
public seminars educated nearly 500 animal advocates in Mumbai and Delhi on how they can take action to stop cruelty.
more than 50 top Indian designers signed our Feather-Free Pledge.

metrics.metric_name: Experts signed letter for rhesus macaque protection

source.quote: More than 60 leading primatologists and wildlife experts signed our letter urging the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change to restore legal protection for rhesus macaques
save more than 30 stranded dogs and buffaloes, along with a cow, in Delhi NCR.
had 30 injured, emaciated horses reportedly used to pull carriages confiscated.

metrics.metric_name: Filmmakers/companies pledged to use non-animal means

source.quote: Twenty-two filmmakers and content production companies signed PETA India's pledge to use robots, computer-generated imagery, or other non-animal means rather than elephants
given huge, astoundingly lifelike mechanical elephants to 15 temples
We helped save seven camels from sacrifice ahead of Eid
raided North East Sun Circus and seized three doves and a goat who were being illegally forced to perform.
successfully encouraged ISKCON Mayapur in West Bengal to release two other elephants, Bishnupriya and Lakshmipriya
secured the rescue of a 36-year-old elephant named Madhuri

metrics.metric_name: Criminal cases registered against animal experimentation labs

source.quote: PETA India secured the nation's first-ever criminal case registered against an animal experimentation laboratory.

metrics.metric_name: Ban on export of animal products

source.quote: India banned the export of reptile skins and various items of animal fur.
secured a ban on buffalo and bulbul bird fights from the Gauhati High Court.
helped get the place shut down. All the dogs were returned to their homes.
Jharkhand banned the use, sale, and manufacture of 'thorn' bits.

metrics.metric_name: Pharmacy chain stopped selling glue traps

source.quote: persuaded Wellness Forever, India's third-largest pharmacy chain, to stop selling cruel, sticky glue traps.
John Abraham pressed Nepali authorities to scrap the Chitwan Elephant Festival's abusive exhibits
ad in multiple cities featuring Mahatma Gandhi leading an animal parade, resembling the Dandi Salt March, with the message, "Support Nonviolence for All - Strengthen Animal Protection Laws."
full-page Times of India advertisement reminded residents of Mumbai and Delhi that Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam ("the world is one family") and of the importance of jivdaya.
donated a life-size mechanical elephant to ISKCON Kharghar in Navi Mumbai
Nagpur officials nabbed hundreds of kilograms of banned manja - razor-sharp kite string - protecting countless birds and other animals from being cut or killed.
